lastUpdatedNote: Seeded from research on 27 Jun 2026. The automation script appends new monthly entries every 5 hours once deployed.

2024-01: ISRO launched XPoSat, India's first X-ray polarimeter satellite to study X-ray sources in space (1 Jan).
ISRO's Aditya-L1 spacecraft successfully entered its final orbit around the Sun-Earth L1 point, completing India's first solar mission (6 Jan).
PM Modi inaugurated the Mumbai Trans Harbour Link (Atal Setu), India's longest sea bridge (12 Jan).
The Ram Mandir at Ayodhya was consecrated in the Pran Pratishtha ceremony led by PM Modi (22 Jan).
France's President Emmanuel Macron was chief guest at India's 75th Republic Day parade (26 Jan).
Bihar CM Nitish Kumar exited the INDIA bloc and was sworn in again as CM after joining the NDA (28 Jan).
2024-02: Uttarakhand became the first Indian state to pass a Uniform Civil Code (UCC) bill (7 Feb).
The Supreme Court struck down the Electoral Bond Scheme as unconstitutional (15 Feb).
ISRO launched the INSAT-3DS meteorological satellite (17 Feb).
Seven of eight Indian Navy veterans released from death-sentence detention in Qatar returned to India (12 Feb).
2024-03: The government notified rules for implementing the Citizenship Amendment Act, 2019 (11 Mar).
The Election Commission published State Bank of India's electoral bond disclosure data (14 Mar).
Chief Election Commissioner Rajiv Kumar announced the schedule for the 2024 Lok Sabha general election (16 Mar).
Delhi CM Arvind Kejriwal was arrested by the Enforcement Directorate in the excise policy case (21 Mar).
2024-04: Polling for the 2024 Lok Sabha general election began in its first of seven phases (19 Apr–1 Jun).
The BJP released its election manifesto, 'Sankalp Patra' (14 Apr).
2024-05: A storm-related hoarding collapse in Ghatkopar, Mumbai killed 17 people (13 May).
Cyclone Remal caused heavy rain and casualties after making landfall in West Bengal (26 May).
IMD recorded a maximum temperature of 52.3°C at Mungeshpur, Delhi, the city's highest ever reading (29 May).
2024-06: Results of the 2024 Lok Sabha election were declared; the BJP-led NDA won a majority (4 Jun).
Narendra Modi was sworn in for a third consecutive term as Prime Minister (9 Jun).
The 18th Lok Sabha was constituted; Om Birla was re-elected Speaker (26–27 Jun).
India won the 2024 ICC Men's T20 World Cup, defeating South Africa in the final (29 Jun).
2024-07: Three new criminal codes — Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, Bharatiya Nagarik Suraksha Sanhita, and Bharatiya Sakshya Adhiniyam — came into force, replacing the colonial-era IPC, CrPC and Evidence Act (1 Jul).
The Union Budget 2024-25 was presented in Parliament.
The Charaideo Maidam in Assam was inscribed as a UNESCO World Heritage Site (26 Jul).
Manu Bhaker won bronze in 10m air pistol shooting at the Paris Olympics, becoming the first Indian woman to win an Olympic shooting medal (28 Jul).
2024-08: At the Paris 2024 Olympics, India won 6 medals: Manu Bhaker (bronze, individual & mixed-team 10m air pistol), the men's hockey team (bronze), Neeraj Chopra (silver, javelin throw), Swapnil Kusale (bronze, 50m rifle 3-positions) and Aman Sehrawat (bronze, wrestling 57kg).
India's 78th Independence Day was celebrated on 15 August.
Devastating landslides struck Wayanad, Kerala, killing over 230 people (30 Jul–early Aug).
The rape and murder of a trainee doctor at R.G. Kar Medical College, Kolkata sparked nationwide protests over hospital safety (9 Aug).
2024-09: The Tripura Peace Accord was signed, ending a 35-year insurgency in the state (4 Sep).
UPSC cancelled the candidature of trainee IAS officer Puja Khedkar over fraud allegations (9 Sep).
2024-10: The BJP retained power in Haryana for a record third consecutive term (5–8 Oct).
J&K held its first assembly election since the 2019 reorganisation; an INC-JKNC alliance won a majority (18 Sep–6 Oct).
India and China agreed on patrolling arrangements along the Line of Actual Control (21 Oct).
2024-11: India successfully test-fired its first long-range hypersonic missile from Abdul Kalam Island, Odisha (16 Nov).
The JMM-led alliance won the Jharkhand assembly election and the BJP-led Mahayuti won a landslide in Maharashtra (20–23 Nov).
Sanjiv Khanna was sworn in as the 51st Chief Justice of India (11 Nov).
2024-12: 18-year-old Gukesh Dommaraju of Chennai became the youngest-ever World Chess Champion (12 Dec).
Sanjay Malhotra took charge as the 26th Governor of the Reserve Bank of India (11 Dec).
A bill on 'One Nation, One Election' (simultaneous elections) was introduced in the Lok Sabha (18 Dec).
Vice President Jagdeep Dhankhar survived a no-confidence motion in the Rajya Sabha (19 Dec).
2025-01: The 2025 Prayag Maha Kumbh Mela began in Prayagraj, eventually drawing over 600 million visitors (13 Jan–26 Feb).
India's first HMPV (Human Metapneumovirus) case was detected in Bengaluru (3 Jan).
The Sonamarg Tunnel in Jammu & Kashmir was inaugurated (13 Jan).
A stampede at the Tirumala Venkateswara Temple killed at least six people (8 Jan).
2025-02: The Union Budget 2025-26 raised the income-tax exemption limit to ₹12 lakh under the new tax regime (1 Feb).
The BJP won a two-thirds majority in the Delhi assembly election, ending AAP's decade-long rule (5 Feb); Rekha Gupta became Delhi's new CM (19 Feb).
Gyanesh Kumar took over as the new Chief Election Commissioner of India (19 Feb).
President's Rule was imposed in Manipur amid continuing ethnic violence (13 Feb).
At least 18 people died in a crowd crush at New Delhi railway station amid Kumbh-related rush (15 Feb).
2025-03: India won the 2025 ICC Champions Trophy for a record third time, beating New Zealand in the final in Dubai (9 Mar).
The Maha Kumbh Mela in Prayagraj concluded after being described as the largest human gathering in history (26 Feb).
2025-04: The Waqf (Amendment) Act, 2025 came into effect (5 Apr).
The government announced that caste enumeration would be included in the next Census (30 Apr).
A terrorist attack in Pahalgam, Jammu & Kashmir killed 26 tourists, mostly Hindu pilgrims (22 Apr).
India signed a deal to buy 26 Rafale-Marine fighter jets from France for the Navy (28 Apr).
2025-05: India launched 'Operation Sindoor', striking terror infrastructure in Pakistan in response to the Pahalgam attack; a ceasefire followed after brief hostilities (7–10 May).
Virat Kohli announced his retirement from Test cricket (12 May).
Karnataka writer Banu Mushtaq won the International Booker Prize for 'Heart Lamp', the first Kannada work to win the award (20 May).
2025-06: Air India Flight 171 (Boeing 787) crashed shortly after takeoff from Ahmedabad, killing 260 people — one of India's deadliest aviation disasters (12 Jun).
The Udhampur–Baramulla railway line was inaugurated, completing rail connectivity to the Kashmir Valley (6 Jun).
Group Captain Shubhanshu Shukla became only the second Indian to travel to space, flying on Axiom Mission 4 (25 Jun).
India and Canada agreed to restore full diplomatic relations after a two-year rift (17 Jun).
2025-07: The Maratha Military Landscapes of India were inscribed as UNESCO World Heritage Sites (11 Jul).
Jagdeep Dhankhar resigned as Vice President of India citing health reasons (21 Jul).
Divya Deshmukh won the Women's Chess World Cup 2025, beating Koneru Humpy in the final (28 Jul).
'Operation Mahadev' killed three militants including the mastermind of the Pahalgam attack (28 Jul).
The US announced a 25% tariff on Indian exports, citing India's continued purchase of Russian oil (31 Jul).
2025-08: India's 79th Independence Day was celebrated on 15 August.
The US raised tariffs on Indian exports to 50%, effective 27 August.
The Promotion and Regulation of Online Gaming Act, 2025 was enacted, banning real-money online gaming in India (22 Aug).
India successfully test-fired the Agni-V intermediate-range ballistic missile (20 Aug).
India and China agreed to resume direct flights and reopen border trade points after a five-year suspension (20 Aug).
2025-09: C. P. Radhakrishnan was elected the 15th Vice President of India and sworn in on 12 September.
The government rolled out 'GST 2.0' reforms simplifying tax slabs amid the US tariff dispute (3 Sep).
The Indian Air Force formally retired its MiG-21 fighter fleet after 62 years of service (26 Sep).
The Supreme Court suspended parts of the Waqf (Amendment) Act, 2025 (15 Sep).
2025-10: Chief Justice of India B. R. Gavai was the target of a shoe-throwing incident in court (6 Oct).
Navi Mumbai International Airport was inaugurated (8 Oct).
India signed a bilateral security and defence agreement with Australia (9 Oct).
Direct flights between India and China resumed for the first time since the COVID-19 pandemic (27 Oct).
2025-11: The BJP-led NDA won a landslide victory (202/243 seats) in the Bihar assembly election; Nitish Kumar took oath as CM for a record 10th time (14–20 Nov).
India won the 2025 Women's Cricket World Cup — its first-ever title in the tournament (2 Nov).
ISRO launched its heaviest-ever communications satellite, CMS-03/GSAT-7R (4,410 kg) (2 Nov).
Surya Kant was sworn in as the 53rd Chief Justice of India (24 Nov).
Kerala was declared the first Indian state to eradicate extreme poverty (1 Nov).
2025-12: Parliament's winter session focused on the aftermath of the Bihar election and continuing debate on electoral roll revisions.
The RBI's Monetary Policy Committee reviewed interest rates amid the ongoing US tariff dispute.
2026-01: At least 14 people died after drinking contaminated water in Indore, Madhya Pradesh (2 Jan).
Nitin Nabin was elected National President of the Bharatiya Janata Party.
Ten soldiers died when an army vehicle fell into a gorge in Doda, Jammu & Kashmir (22 Jan).
Nationwide protests broke out over the UGC Bill, 2026 (26 Jan).
India and the European Union reached a free trade agreement (27 Jan).
Maharashtra Deputy CM Ajit Pawar was among five killed when a Learjet 45 crashed at Baramati Airport (28 Jan).
The Supreme Court stayed implementation of the UGC Equity Regulations, 2026 (29 Jan).
2026-02: The US announced a trade deal cutting tariffs on Indian exports to 18% in exchange for India halting Russian oil purchases (2 Feb).
The 2026 ICC Men's T20 World Cup, co-hosted by India and Sri Lanka, began (7 Feb).
The AI Impact Summit was held in New Delhi (16–20 Feb).
Brazil and India signed an agreement on critical minerals and rare-earth cooperation (21 Feb).
A Delhi court discharged Arvind Kejriwal, Manish Sisodia, K. Kavitha and 22 others in the liquor-policy case (27 Feb).
2026-03: India was awarded hosting rights for the 2028 World Athletics Indoor Championships, to be held in Bhubaneswar — the fourth Asian nation to host the event.
India won the 2026 Men's T20 World Cup, beating New Zealand in the final.
A special NIA court sentenced Kashmiri separatist Asiya Andrabi to life imprisonment (24 Mar).
2026-04: Nitish Kumar resigned as Bihar's Chief Minister after being elected to the Rajya Sabha; his deputy Samrat Chaudhary became Bihar's first-ever BJP Chief Minister (14 Apr).
Assembly elections were held across several Indian states and a union territory, alongside Rajya Sabha and local-body elections (Apr–May).
2026-05: Counting and results were finalised for the 2026 state assembly and local body elections held in April–May.
2026-06: The government temporarily blocked Telegram in India and directed it to disable message-editing until 30 June.
Delhi Police arrested seven operatives of a Pakistan-backed arms and narcotics smuggling syndicate.
At least 15 people died in a fire at a coaching centre in Lucknow (22 Jun).
awards: Padma Awards 2025: 7 Padma Vibhushan, 19 Padma Bhushan, 113 Padma Shri — recipients included R. Ashwin, Arijit Singh, D. Nageshwar Reddy and (posthumously) M. T. Vasudevan Nair and Sharda Sinha.
Padma Awards 2026: 131 awards (5 Vibhushan, 13 Bhushan, 113 Shri) — recipients included Rohit Sharma, Mammootty, Alka Yagnik, R. Madhavan and Vijay Amritraj.

Nobel Prizes 2024: Physics — Hopfield & Hinton (machine learning); Chemistry — Baker, Hassabis & Jumper (protein structure prediction); Medicine — Ambros & Ruvkun (microRNA); Literature — Han Kang; Peace — Nihon Hidankyo.
Banu Mushtaq (India) won the 2025 International Booker Prize for 'Heart Lamp' — first Kannada-language work to win.

2000: Karnam Malleswari won bronze in weightlifting at the Sydney Olympics, becoming the first Indian woman to win an Olympic medal.
The National Population Policy 2000 was announced.
Vajpayee government continued IT and economic liberalisation push.
2001: A devastating earthquake struck Bhuj, Gujarat on 26 January, killing nearly 20,000 people.
The Agra Summit was held between PM Vajpayee and Pakistan's President Musharraf.
Terrorists attacked the Indian Parliament in New Delhi on 13 December.
Census 2001 recorded India's population at about 1.02 billion.
2002: The Godhra train burning (27 Feb) triggered the Gujarat riots.
A. P. J. Abdul Kalam became the 11th President of India (July 2002).
India participated in the 2002 Commonwealth Games in Manchester.
2003: India reached the final of the 2003 Cricket World Cup, finishing runner-up to Australia.
India and Pakistan agreed to a ceasefire along the Line of Control (Nov 2003).
The Fiscal Responsibility and Budget Management Act was enacted.
2004: The Indian Ocean tsunami (26 Dec) devastated Tamil Nadu, Andhra Pradesh and the Andaman & Nicobar Islands.
The UPA, led by the Congress, won the general election; Manmohan Singh became India's first Sikh Prime Minister.
The National Rural Employment Guarantee scheme was conceptualised.
2005: The Right to Information Act was passed, strengthening citizens' access to government information.
The National Rural Employment Guarantee Act (NREGA) was enacted.
A powerful earthquake struck Jammu & Kashmir (Oct 2005), also affecting Pakistan-administered Kashmir.
Mumbai suffered record flooding in July 2005.
2006: Serial bomb blasts hit Mumbai's suburban trains, killing over 180 people (11 Jul).
India and the US held early negotiations on the civil nuclear cooperation deal.
Reliance Industries became one of India's most valuable companies.
2007: Pratibha Patil became India's first woman President.
Serial bombings struck Hyderabad and the Samjhauta Express train.
India's GDP growth rate touched around 9%, among the fastest in the world.
2008: Terrorists carried out coordinated attacks in Mumbai, killing over 160 people (26 Nov).
Abhinav Bindra won India's first individual Olympic gold medal, in 10m air rifle shooting at Beijing.
The India-US civil nuclear agreement (123 Agreement) was signed.
Tata Motors launched the Nano, marketed as the world's cheapest car.
2009: The UPA was re-elected in the general election; Manmohan Singh began a second term as PM.
The Unique Identification Authority of India (UIDAI) was set up, laying the groundwork for Aadhaar.
India responded to the global H1N1 swine flu outbreak.
2010: India hosted the Commonwealth Games in New Delhi.
The 2G spectrum allocation scam came to light.
India formally adopted the rupee symbol (₹), designed by D. Udaya Kumar.
2011: India won the 2011 Cricket World Cup, hosted at home, with M. S. Dhoni as captain.
Anna Hazare led a high-profile anti-corruption movement demanding a strong Lokpal Bill.
Census 2011 recorded India's population at about 1.21 billion.
2012: The Delhi gang-rape case ('Nirbhaya', 16 Dec) triggered nationwide protests and the Justice Verma Committee on women's safety.
India advanced preparations for its first interplanetary mission, the Mars Orbiter Mission.
2013: India launched the Mars Orbiter Mission (Mangalyaan), its first interplanetary mission (Nov 2013).
Devastating floods struck Uttarakhand in June 2013.
The Criminal Law (Amendment) Act, 2013 ('Nirbhaya Act') was passed, strengthening laws against sexual violence.
2014: Narendra Modi became Prime Minister after the BJP won a full majority in the Lok Sabha election.
Telangana was formed as India's 29th state (2 Jun).
Mangalyaan entered Mars orbit on its first attempt (24 Sep), a global first.
The Swachh Bharat Abhiyan and Make in India initiatives, and the Pradhan Mantri Jan Dhan Yojana, were launched.
2015: The United Nations adopted 21 June as the first International Day of Yoga, an initiative led by India.
India signed the Land Boundary Agreement with Bangladesh.
The Smart Cities Mission and AIIB (India as a founding member) were launched.
2016: The government demonetised ₹500 and ₹1000 notes overnight (8 Nov).
India conducted surgical strikes on terror launch pads across the LoC after the Uri attack.
P. V. Sindhu (silver, badminton) and Sakshi Malik (bronze, wrestling) won medals at the Rio Olympics.
2017: The Goods and Services Tax (GST) was implemented nationwide from 1 July, unifying India's indirect tax system.
Ram Nath Kovind became the 14th President of India.
India and China faced a military standoff at Doklam.
2018: Ayushman Bharat (PM-JAY) was launched as the world's largest government-funded health insurance scheme.
The Supreme Court decriminalised consensual same-sex relations by reading down Section 377.
Kerala suffered its worst floods in nearly a century.
2019: Article 370 was abrogated, revoking Jammu & Kashmir's special status and reorganising it into two union territories (5 Aug).
India conducted the Balakot airstrike after the Pulwama terror attack.
The BJP-led NDA was re-elected with a bigger majority in the general election.
The Supreme Court ruled in favour of the Ram temple in the Ayodhya land dispute case (Nov 2019).
Chandrayaan-2 was launched; its Vikram lander crash-landed on the Moon's surface.
2020: The COVID-19 pandemic led to a nationwide lockdown from 25 March.
Twenty Indian soldiers were killed in the Galwan Valley clash with China (Jun 2020).
Three farm laws were passed (later repealed in 2021) and the National Education Policy (NEP) 2020 was approved.
The Ram Mandir 'bhoomi pujan' (ground-breaking ceremony) was held in Ayodhya.
2021: India faced a devastating second wave of COVID-19 (Apr–May 2021).
The three farm laws were repealed by Parliament (Nov 2021).
Neeraj Chopra won gold in javelin throw at the Tokyo Olympics — India's first individual Olympic gold in athletics.
India's COVID-19 vaccination drive (Covaxin and Covishield) expanded nationwide.
2022: Droupadi Murmu became India's first tribal woman President.
India marked 75 years of independence with the 'Azadi Ka Amrit Mahotsav' and 'Har Ghar Tiranga' campaigns.
India evacuated thousands of citizens from Ukraine under 'Operation Ganga' amid the Russia-Ukraine war.
India's economy became the world's 5th largest by nominal GDP.
2023: Chandrayaan-3 achieved a successful soft landing near the Moon's south pole (23 Aug) — now marked as National Space Day.
India hosted the G20 Summit in New Delhi (Sep 2023).
The Nari Shakti Vandan Adhiniyam (Women's Reservation Bill) was passed, reserving 33% of seats for women in the Lok Sabha and state assemblies.
India's new Parliament building was inaugurated (May 2023).
India recorded its best-ever medal tally (107 medals) at the Asian Games in Hangzhou.
